title: campanula versicolor subsp. korabensis
intro: Campanula versicolor subsp. korabensis, also known as Korab bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family, Campanulaceae. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Traditionally, Korab bellflower is prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system.
